ending: Serena loses the execution match to Celeste, but deliberately removes Jasmine from danger before her own shield breaks. Ashlock then transforms Serena into a sacred tree rather than ending her existence. She retains her soul and Abyssal Tide affinity, reaches Star Core during her activation, and becomes the flying bastion and designated protector of Ashfallen City. Stella remains a seventh-stage Star Core Ether cultivator under concealment, with the Azure clan aware of her identity and the fate of the Ether Origin Stone unresolved. Nox controls Tartarus and its Shadow Key Law, while Hades recovers there. Ashlock holds fourth-stage Nascent Soul cultivation, a growing inner world, and a worship-driven network that now extends through the public All-Seeing Eye. Diana, Elaine, Douglas, Larry, Evelyn, Jasmine, Sam, and the Redclaw allies remain with Ashfallen. The next emergency is already arriving: failed spiritual springs have driven an early beast tide south, and Vincent's summit may split the Blood Lotus sect over whether to flee. Stella's father, the Celestial Order, the suffering World Tree, Morrigan's proposed struggle against the recurring cycle, and the Azure clan all remain unresolved.
in_short: Ashlock completes his Nascent Soul ascent, develops an inner world powered by worship, and expands Ashfallen through the All-Seeing Eye and a mass Mystic Realm expedition. Valandor secretly helps conceal Stella from Vincent, while Stella learns her father may be alive and that the Celestial Order is hunting her. Morrigan reveals herself as the Origin of Void and describes a recurring cycle binding the origins to the World Tree. In the Mystic Realm, Nox claims Tartarus and Shadow Key Law, while Stella infiltrates the Azure clan, gains Ether affinity, loses the repaired Origin Stone, and exposes her identity. Ashfallen returns stronger and stages a recruitment tournament. Serena Blacktide kills Old Bill, attacks Sam, and plants an egg that drains the trading company's vault. After Celeste defeats her in a public judgment match, Serena is transformed into a Star Core sacred tree and Ashfallen City's Abyssal Tide bastion. The volume closes with an early beast tide approaching and Vincent's sect near division over evacuation.

recaps.text: Previously: Ashlock was reborn as a demonic tree and built Ashfallen around Red Vine Peak, gathering cultivators, beasts, and transformed trees into an unconventional sect. Stella became his adopted daughter and public representative, with Diana as her closest companion. Elaine and Douglas joined the growing organization, while Larry and Maple served as powerful nonhuman allies. Ashlock's conflict with hostile families expanded his territory into Darklight City and gave Redclaw a dominant regional position. Nox, once an enemy, suffered severe soul damage and was remade as a sacred shadow tree under Ashfallen's control. The Blood Lotus sect remained the greater power over the region. Its patriarch, Vincent Nightrose, coveted rare bloodlines and posed a particular danger to Stella. By the start of this volume, Ashfallen had gained wealth, followers, demonic offspring, and new cultivation opportunities, but its strength still depended on secrecy. Ashlock was approaching Nascent Soul, Stella's origins remained uncertain, and confrontation with the powers above Darklight City had only been delayed.

Vincent Nightrose senses an exceptional eastern ascension and learns that Redclaw now controls Darklight City. He continues prolonging his damaged vessel by consuming bloodlines and orders Valandor to capture Stella. On Red Vine Peak, Ashlock begins his own concealed ascent to Nascent Soul and discovers an inner world forming around nine moons. Stella and Larry enter it while he sorts accumulated resources. Valandor reaches Ashfallen but reveals that he is not acting as Vincent's loyal hunter. Stella's father once freed him from Vincent's control with World Tree sap and charged him to protect Stella. Valandor proposes staging Stella and Diana's deaths during the Voidmind conflict. Stella visits the Eternal Pursuit Pavilion, collects a bounty, and learns Ashfallen has been mistakenly classified as a divine organization. Records point to another Crestfallen cultivator in the Frozen Star Sect, possibly her father. Tiberius sends him a message, but the only reply is a warning that people are coming for Stella. Her parentage, the identity of her pursuers, and the danger to Ashlock's unfinished ascension remain unresolved.

Elaine returns to the Voidmind residence with Valandor and Larry. Ashlock prevents escape, Larry defeats the family's Void techniques, and Elaine's father completes his transformation into a Void Tree after a failed soul attack. Elaine kills the elder who helped displace her branch, then reunites with her mother, Morrigan. Ashlock takes control of the residence, preserves its outward defenses, and develops Void Storm Aegis by exposing his soul to captured Void attacks. Stella and Diana shelter inside his forming inner world when the warning from the Frozen Star Sect makes pursuit seem imminent. Ashlock completes his ascent to Nascent Soul. His stable inner world contains a divine flesh tree that functions as an infant second soul, and worship now produces divine energy and sacrificial credits. The system becomes conversational but refuses to explain its origin or Ashlock's rebirth until all nine moons are unlocked. Ashlock also hears the World Tree in pain after drawing heavily on its power. Seeking both strength and public legitimacy, he assigns Stella to organize temples, pill distribution, and worship of the All-Seeing Eye while she remains hidden from those hunting her.

Ashfallen expands on several fronts. Choi suppresses reports from Ashfallen City while Douglas, Elaine, Diana, and other oath-bound members prepare for a mass Mystic Realm expedition. Morrigan asks for help concealing the disappearance of the mortals around her academy, and Ashlock chooses to supply rescued servants rather than eliminate the isolated nobles. Evelyn meets Nox and learns that the sacred shadow tree retains her sister's memories but was changed by soul damage. Nox accepts responsibility for her former actions, and Evelyn forgives her. The first public All-Seeing Eye service then brings mortal recruits into Ashlock's inner world. Sam, a village youth previously helped by Stella, is among those newly enabled to cultivate. Celestial Order inquisitors attempt to execute Stella during the ceremony, but Ashlock destroys them with Void Lightning. Stella rejects the Order as a home and affirms Ashlock's inner world as the place she belongs, while still intending to save her mother and the World Tree. Ashlock promises to accompany her to the Celestial Empire. The service gives his faith network a public beginning and sharply increases the divine energy available to strengthen Ashfallen.

Ashlock opens the Mystic Realm to his followers. Jasmine enters the poisonous Secret Garden of Venomwood with Sol as protection, while Stella pursues her own advancement. Evelyn lands in the much more dangerous Shadow Veil Sanctum, prompting Ashlock and Nox to enter through a bastion. They kill an Eclipse Behemoth, find Evelyn unharmed, and continue toward the Dark Throne. Morrigan appears inside the realm and resists every attempt Ashlock makes to dominate her. She identifies herself as the Origin of Void and explains that origins are primordial embodiments of affinities. According to her, cultivators and the World Tree bound the origins to a cycle in which the tree's death forces them to die and reincarnate without their former cultivation. She believes Ashlock's divinity may offer a way out and suspects that he is also an origin, though this is not confirmed. No pact is formed. Elsewhere, Stella is transported into an Azure clan tournament realm, kills two clan members, takes their clothing, and begins an infiltration. Ashlock now faces both the immediate dangers of the expedition and a much larger conflict involving the World Tree, heaven, and the origins' recurring deaths.

Stella replaces Lyra, a masked Azure clan disciple, and enters the tournament under her identity. She kills Lyra's brother, wins the main bracket, and is taken before Elder Vortexian. He recognizes the substitution but assumes Stella has powerful backing and asks her to repair a damaged Origin Stone left by the deceased Heavenly King Cepheus. Stella reveals her Crestfallen name and stakes her family legacy on the task. The celestial library subjects her to an accelerated and painful transfer of knowledge. In Venomwood, Jasmine forms a Nature core, survives poisonous plant attacks with Sol's aid, and begins absorbing Poison Key. In the Shadow Veil Sanctum, Ashlock, Nox, and Evelyn defeat a lich guarding the Spectral Tombs. Ashlock remakes it as a powerful Shadow Lich Ent, then starts transforming the remains hidden inside a colossal Shadow Sovereign statue. Nox explains that her tree body and shadow avatar divide one soul, allowing one half to survive the other's destruction at the cost of memories and personality. Ashlock has gained formidable servants, but the Dark Throne remains beyond them and the Mystic Realm's time limit is approaching.

Ashlock names his new shadow titan Hades and the Shadow Lich Anubis. They reach the Dark Throne, but its controlled whirlpool of Shadow Key traps Hades and threatens Nox. Ashlock nearly destroys his soul by carrying too much Shadow Key back to his main body, then recovers at fourth-stage Nascent Soul. Morrigan massacres senior Skyrend cultivators while wearing All-Seeing Eye clothing, accidentally generating enough worship for Ashlock to assimilate the throne into Nox's bastion. Nox gains Shadow Key Law and ownership of the conquered realm, which Ashlock names Tartarus. Stella repairs the Ether Origin Stone through Crestfallen knowledge, attunes to Ether Key, and attempts to take the stone. Maple saves her from Vortexian's domain, but the elder escapes with the remnant and reports Stella to the Azure clan. The expedition ends with broad gains across Ashfallen. Stella and Diana return at seventh-stage Star Core, Evelyn reaches sixth stage, and Ryker reaches Silver Corps. Jasmine returns in an altered state after her poisonous ordeal. Ashfallen is substantially stronger, but the Azure clan now knows Stella's identity and Morrigan's actions have publicly implicated the cult in a major massacre.

Jasmine's merged plant soul has left her with a Nature core, poisonous blood, and a body able to incorporate poison into her attacks. Ashlock decides Vincent must eventually be removed and begins preparing both for war and for a recruitment tournament inside his inner world. Three Redclaw youths who concealed the deaths of their companions are transformed into trees as punishment. Elsewhere, collapsing spiritual springs wake Astralis and drive an early beast tide south, three years ahead of expectation. Ashlock and Douglas complete a floating tournament settlement while Elder Mo continues forging an artifact intended to conceal Stella's bloodline. Nox organizes Tartarus into controlled shadow-cultivation rooms. Sam, driven by ambition after public humiliation, uses a tainted beast core to force a rapid Soul Fire breakthrough. Jasmine struggles with sword training, so Stella redirects her toward close combat and the delivery of her paralyzing poison. Stella also demonstrates her new Ether movement by displacing Ryker after he defeats Amber in a spar. The tournament is ready to begin, but Ashfallen's rapid growth coincides with the first signs of a regional monster crisis.

Ashlock defines the recruitment tournament as a search for potential rather than simple victory. Stella's concealment amulet works, and she begins training as an assassin while Elder Mo completes the vine weapons. Serena Blacktide arrives seeking her sect's stolen stone. She cuts one of Ashlock's roots for cursed sap and kills Old Bill when he objects, leaving a damaged offspring tree as evidence. She enters the tournament and joins the All-Seeing Eye for access to discounted pills. Stella investigates the root attack while unexpected crowds overwhelm Ashfallen's organizers. Serena hides her cultivation, adopts the name Daisy, and befriends Jasmine. During a public dispute over Ashfallen's motives, Stella executes a critic and reinforces the sect's feared reputation. Serena avoids immediate suspicion, then invents a missing sister when Stella questions her. Celeste Starweaver arrives to test the arena shields against cosmic attacks. Serena flees the tournament area, buys every pill the Trading Company worker has available, and secretly gives an employee an abyssal-creature egg intended to cause havoc. She plans to silence witnesses, leaving Stella's investigation and the tournament exposed to a threat from within Ashfallen's own network.

Serena attacks Sam after he recognizes her, but he survives and Stella rescues him. The egg in the Trading Company hatches into a dark tide devourer, drains the vault's spirit stones, and submits to Stella. Stella captures Serena and obtains her confession to killing Old Bill, damaging Ashlock's root, attacking Sam, and planting the egg. Ashfallen turns her sentence into a public match. Celeste and Sam face Serena and Jasmine under protective shields. Jasmine defeats Sam with her poisoned whip, while Celeste steadily exhausts Serena. Serena refuses a chance to fake a betrayal, but then removes Jasmine from the arena in a way that saves her from Celeste's attack. Celeste defeats Serena. Ashlock carries out the announced tree transformation before the gathered cult, preserving Serena's soul and Abyssal Tide affinity. During activation she reaches Star Core and becomes a flying bastion shaped as a tree rising from a bowl of water. She is assigned to protect Ashfallen City. The judgment strengthens belief in the All-Seeing Eye, but the closing news redirects attention outward: Celeste's divination confirms an approaching beast tide, and Vincent's summit may descend into civil war over whether the Blood Lotus sect should evacuate.
